questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Nearest Centroid Classifier?
options: A) To minimize the overall distance to the nearest class centroid
B) To maximize the number of nearest neighbors
C) To find the most similar class in the feature space
D) To reduce the dimensionality of the feature space
questions.explanation: The primary goal of the Nearest Centroid Classifier is to find the class with the closest centroid to the input data point.

questions.question: What is the main difference between Nearest Centroid Classifier and Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm?
options: A) The Nearest Centroid Classifier uses weights for the k-nearest neighbors
B) The Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm uses the centroid of each class as a weight
C) The Nearest Centroid Classifier uses the centroid of each class as a weight
D) The Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm uses the distance of each class centroid from the input data point
questions.explanation: The main difference between the two is that the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm uses the centroid of each class as a weight, whereas the Nearest Centroid Classifier uses the centroid of each class as a final prediction.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using the Nearest Centroid Classifier in high-dimensional spaces?
options: A) It is more computationally efficient
B) It is less prone to overfitting
C) It is more robust to noise in the data
D) It is scalable to high-dimensional spaces
questions.explanation: The Nearest Centroid Classifier is more robust to noise in the data because the centroid is less affected by outliers compared to other classification methods.

questions.question: Can the Nearest Centroid Classifier be used for classification problems with non-linearly separable data?
options: A) Yes, it can be used for non-linearly separable data using kernel functions
B) No, it cannot be used for non-linearly separable data
C) It depends on the type of kernel used
D) It is only suitable for linearly separable data
questions.explanation: The Nearest Centroid Classifier can be used for non-linearly separable data using kernel functions, such as the radial basis function (RBF) kernel.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of the Nearest Centroid Classifier?
options: A) O(n log n) due to the sorting of the k-nearest neighbors
B) O(n log n) due to the computation of the class centroids
C) O(n) due to the computation of the k-nearest neighbors
D) O(n^2) due to the computation of the distances between the input data point and the class centroids
questions.explanation: The time complexity of the Nearest Centroid Classifier is O(n log n) due to the sorting of the k-nearest neighbors.
